mnemonics: ULTIMATUM: Understand Last Take-it-or-leave-it, In Many Actual Transactions, Usually Money's unfair
keyPoints: A two-player sequential game where Player 1 (Proposer) offers a split of a sum, and Player 2 (Responder) can only accept or reject the entire offer.
The Rational Equilibrium prediction (Subgame Perfect Nash Equilibrium) is for the Proposer to offer the smallest possible amount and the Responder to accept it, as something is better than nothing.

Experimental results consistently show that humans deviate from the rational equilibrium; low offers are often rejected due to perceptions of unfairness, and proposers often make fair offers anticipating this.
commonMistakes: Assuming players are purely rational and self-interested, ignoring the role of fairness, reciprocity, and emotion in decision-making.
Believing the Responder has bargaining power or can make a counter-offer; their only power is to veto the deal, destroying the entire sum.
